system.description:
Discipline: Dianxue
Skill: Arm Strike -1d10 twice against Parry
Type: Normal
Qi: 5

This fearsome Technique allows the user to seal another Kung Fu user’s Techniques, preventing her from using them. You jab quickly and carefully at the target’s temple, then her heart, blocking the intellectual and physical ability to use one Technique she knows of your choice. First, make Arm Strike against Parry at the target’s temple (this is made at -1d10, per the specific targeting rules). On a Success, you choose one Kung Fu Technique that she knows, and she is intellectually unable to use that. However, if the Technique you choose is a Counter, each time it is applicable she may make an Endurance roll TN 7 + your Ranks in Dianxue to use the Counter as a reaction of muscle memory. To prevent this, you may make a subsequent jab to her heart as part of the same action, once again with an Arm Strike at -1d10 penalty. If you hit, the Technique is also sealed within her heart, so she may not use it at all and is not afforded the above Endurance roll. If your Arm Strike misses, the Qi energy you have summoned has no outlet, and so it turns on you, sealing one of your Kung Fu Techniques (chosen randomly by the GM). The Technique is sealed for one hour, or one day on a Total Success. Cathartic: When used Cathartically the duration is permanent, but so are the Imbalance Points you take for using this. Note: If you fail, losing one of your own Techniques, the duration is still permanent.
system.source: Wandering Heroes of Ogre Gate, Chapter 3: Kung Fu Techniques
system.catharticEffect: When used Cathartically the duration is permanent, but so are the Imbalance Points you take for using this. Note: If you fail, losing one of your own Techniques, the duration is still permanent.
